name: Chart
description: Declarative chart wrapper over ECharts — 30+ types supported.

api.name: Chart
api.description: Declarative chart wrapper over ECharts — 30+ types supported.

parameters.name: Option
parameters.description: Typed ECharts option to render. Ignored when OptionJson is set.

parameters.name: OptionJson
parameters.description: Raw ECharts option as a JSON string, used instead of Option when set — an escape hatch for option shapes not covered by the typed model.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Declarative children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Other content renders nothing visible.

parameters.name: Theme
parameters.description: ECharts theme name (or registered custom theme) applied on init; changing it disposes and reinitializes the underlying chart instance.

parameters.name: EChartsSource
parameters.description: URL of the ECharts UMD bundle to load. Falls back to the library-bundled version when not set.

parameters.name: AriaLabel
parameters.description: Accessible text alternative for the chart (WCAG 1.1.1). When set, the canvas host is exposed as role="img" with this aria-label so screen readers announce a summary instead of an empty graphic. Strongly recommended.

parameters.name: AccessibilityLayer
parameters.description: When true (default, matching shadcn's accessibilityLayer), the chart renders a visually-hidden data table built from the series data and makes the canvas host keyboard-focusable with a summarising aria-label, so screen-reader and keyboard users can reach the otherwise opaque <canvas> data. When AriaLabel is not set, a generated summary of the data is used as the label. Set to false to opt out entirely.

parameters.name: MaxAccessibilityRows
parameters.description: Upper bound on the number of data rows the hidden screen-reader table materialises (default DefaultMaxAccessibilityRows). A big series would otherwise emit one hidden <tr> per data point — thousands of rows of DOM bloat that no assistive tech can usefully navigate. Above the cap the table keeps the first N rows plus a single "… and N more data points" row; the host's summarising aria-label is built from the FULL data so its totals stay accurate. Set to 0 (or negative) to disable the cap and render every row.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: CSS width of the chart container. Defaults to 100%.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: CSS height of the chart container. Defaults to 350px.

parameters.name: OnClick
parameters.description: Invoked with the ECharts click-event payload when the user clicks a chart element.

parameters.name: OnMouseOver
parameters.description: Invoked with the ECharts mouseover-event payload when the pointer hovers a chart element.

parameters.name: ShowLoading
parameters.description: When true, shows ECharts' built-in loading spinner overlay — distinct from the skeleton driven by IsLoading.

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: Chart group name used to sync cursor/zoom interactions across multiple charts sharing the same group.

parameters.name: AspectRatio
parameters.description: CSS aspect-ratio applied to the container instead of a fixed Height (e.g. 16/9).

parameters.name: OnDoubleClick
parameters.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user double-clicks a chart element.

parameters.name: OnBrushSelected
parameters.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user completes a brush selection.

parameters.name: OnDataZoom
parameters.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user zooms or pans via data zoom.

parameters.name: OnInitError
parameters.description: Invoked with the error message when ECharts fails to initialize (e.g. an invalid EChartsSource).

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS class(es) applied to the chart's root element.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the chart's root element.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, forces the loading state on top of the chart (consumer is refetching data). Independent of the JS-driven ECharts loading indicator controlled by ShowLoading.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders the loading state while the chart has not yet rendered for the first time, and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out entirely.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the skeleton. Each chart wrapper presets a sensible default (bars for Bar, pie ring for Pie, etc.) — consumers only override when they want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with synthesized placeholder data in a muted palette; ECharts animates the option swap to real data. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG overlay skeleton.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds — each tick regenerates the placeholder dataset with a new random seed so bars/lines/slices visibly redistribute while loading. Defaults to 1400ms; clamped to ≥200ms.

parameters.name: PhantomShapeOverride
parameters.description: Explicit override for the phantom layout (category count, series count, stacking, area fill). When null (default), the shape is inferred from the real Option so the phantom mirrors the actual chart structure.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), overlays a small top-right pill containing a spinner and LoadingText on top of the active skeleton — a clear, always-on visual cue that the chart is loading. The pill renders independently of SkeletonStyle so phantom and silhouette both get the same hint. Set to false for bare visuals where the skeleton speaks for itself.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to null or empty to hide the text and show only the spinner. When not set, uses the active locale's "Chart.Loading" string (default: "Loading…").

events.name: OnClick
events.description: Invoked with the ECharts click-event payload when the user clicks a chart element.

events.name: OnMouseOver
events.description: Invoked with the ECharts mouseover-event payload when the pointer hovers a chart element.

events.name: OnDoubleClick
events.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user double-clicks a chart element.

events.name: OnBrushSelected
events.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user completes a brush selection.

events.name: OnDataZoom
events.description: Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user zooms or pans via data zoom.

events.name: OnInitError
events.description: Invoked with the error message when ECharts fails to initialize (e.g. an invalid EChartsSource).

enums.name: ChartLabelStrategy
enums.description: Strategy for rendering category-axis labels on cartesian charts.

enums.name: ChartSkeletonKind
enums.description: Shape family for ChartSkeleton. Each value renders a different SVG silhouette that matches the shape of an actual chart, so loading states don't cause layout shift or empty whitespace flicker before ECharts mounts.

enums.name: ChartSkeletonStyle
enums.description: How the loading state's visual is presented. Phantom = render the real chart with synthesized data in a muted palette and morph to real data via ECharts' option-change animation (default — feels continuous). Silhouette = fall back to the legacy SVG skeleton overlay. A small "Loading…" pill (spinner + label) is overlaid on TOP of whichever style is chosen via ShowLoadingLabel so users never mistake the cycling phantom data for a bug. The label can be disabled per-chart for bare visuals.

enums.name: ChartThresholdAxis
enums.description: Which axis a threshold is perpendicular to. Horizontal = a yAxis line spanning the chart width; Vertical = an xAxis line spanning the chart height.

parameters.name: Categories
parameters.description: Category labels along the axis, one per data point; series values are matched to these by index.

parameters.name: Series
parameters.description: The series to plot, each with a name and its list of values aligned to Categories by index.

parameters.name: Smooth
parameters.description: When true, renders the line using a smoothed (curved) interpolation instead of straight segments between points.

parameters.name: ShowDots
parameters.description: When true (default), draws a marker dot at each data point on the line.

parameters.name: Stacked
parameters.description: When true, stacks each series on top of the previous one instead of overlaying them independently.

parameters.name: Gradient
parameters.description: When true (default) the fill uses a lighter opacity than a solid area. This is the legacy opacity-only behaviour and is unaffected by GradientFill.

parameters.name: GradientFill
parameters.description: Opt into a real vertical gradient fill: each series' area fades from its own (brand/theme) colour at the top to transparent at the bottom, the look used by modern dashboards. Off by default so existing charts are unchanged. Override the stops with GradientStops.

parameters.name: GradientStops
parameters.description: Explicit gradient colour-stops applied to every series' area fill when set (implies GradientFill). Offsets run 0 (top) → 1 (bottom); colours accept theme tokens like var(--color-primary). When null and GradientFill is true, a per-series colour → transparent fade is used.

parameters.name: ShowLegend
parameters.description: When true (default), renders a legend listing each series/segment name.

parameters.name: ShowTooltip
parameters.description: When true (default), shows a tooltip with the value(s) under the cursor on hover.

parameters.name: Colors
parameters.description: Explicit colors, in series/segment order. Falls back to the active theme's chart palette when not set and ColorPalette is not set.

parameters.name: Width
parameters.description: CSS width of the chart container. Defaults to 100%.

parameters.name: Height
parameters.description: CSS height of the chart container. Defaults to 350px.

parameters.name: Theme
parameters.description: ECharts theme name (or registered custom theme) applied to the chart. Falls back to the library default when not set.

parameters.name: Class
parameters.description: Additional CSS class(es) applied to the chart's root element.

parameters.name: ChildContent
parameters.description: Declarative annotation children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Forwarded to the underlying Chart.

parameters.name: AdditionalAttributes
parameters.description: Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the chart's root element.

parameters.name: IsLoading
parameters.description: When true, overlays a shape-aware loading skeleton on top of the chart (consumer is fetching data). Flips back to the real chart once set to false.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingSkeleton
parameters.description: When true (default), renders a shape-aware skeleton while the chart has not yet mounted and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out.

parameters.name: SkeletonKind
parameters.description: Shape family for the loading skeleton. Defaults to ChartSkeletonKind.Area for this chart type; override if you want a different silhouette.

parameters.name: SkeletonStyle
parameters.description: Phantom (default) renders the real chart with placeholder data and morphs to real data via ECharts' option-change animation. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG skeleton overlay.

parameters.name: PhantomCycleMs
parameters.description: Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds. Lower = faster data cycling while loading. Defaults to 1400.

parameters.name: ShowLoadingLabel
parameters.description: When true (default), shows a small "Loading…" pill in the top-right corner whenever IsLoading is active. Works on top of any SkeletonStyle.

parameters.name: LoadingText
parameters.description: Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to empty to hide. Defaults to "Loading…".

parameters.name: ShowDataLabels
parameters.description: When true, renders a label with each data point's value directly on the chart.

parameters.name: LabelPosition
parameters.description: Placement of the value label relative to its data point (e.g. top, inside). Only applies when ShowDataLabels is true.

parameters.name: LabelFormat
parameters.description: ECharts label formatter string/template applied to the value labels (e.g. {c}%). Only applies when ShowDataLabels is true.

parameters.name: LabelStrategy
parameters.description: Controls category-axis label rendering. Smart auto-rotates at higher densities; ShowAll forces all horizontal; Auto thins overlapping labels.

parameters.name: LabelRotate
parameters.description: Manual rotation override (degrees) for category-axis labels.

parameters.name: DataZoom
parameters.description: When true, adds a zoom/pan slider (plus inside wheel-zoom) so users can navigate large datasets.

parameters.name: Toolbox
parameters.description: When true, shows the ECharts toolbox (save-as-image, restore, data-zoom) in the corner of the chart.

parameters.name: ColorPalette
parameters.description: Explicit ordered color palette applied to the chart's series/segments; takes precedence over Colors when both are set.

parameters.name: AnimationDuration
parameters.description: Duration (in milliseconds) of the chart's entrance/update animation. Uses ECharts' default when not set.

parameters.name: AnimationEasing
parameters.description: ECharts easing function name (e.g. cubicOut) used for the chart's entrance/update animation.

parameters.name: MarkLine
parameters.description: Optional mark-line configuration (e.g. average/threshold lines) applied to the first series.

parameters.name: MarkPoint
parameters.description: Optional mark-point configuration (e.g. max/min markers) applied to the first series.

parameters.name: MarkArea
parameters.description: Optional mark-area configuration (e.g. shaded threshold bands) applied to the first series.

parameters.description: Controls how category-axis labels are rendered. Smart (default): show every label; auto-rotate -30° at 11–16 categories, -60° at 17–24, -75° above 24. mdSummary:
# Chart

Declarative chart wrapper over ECharts — 30+ types supported.

**Package:** `Lumeo.Charts` · **Category:** Data Display/Charts
**Docs:** https://lumeo.nativ.sh/components/chart

## Parameters
| Owner | Name | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Chart | Option | `EChartOption?` | `` | Typed ECharts option to render. Ignored when OptionJson is set. |
| Chart | OptionJson | `string?` | `` | Raw ECharts option as a JSON string, used instead of Option when set — an escape hatch for option shapes not covered by the typed model. |
| Chart | ChildContent | `RenderFragment?` | `` | Declarative children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Other content renders nothing visible. |
| Chart | Theme | `string?` | `` | ECharts theme name (or registered custom theme) applied on init; changing it disposes and reinitializes the underlying chart instance. |
| Chart | EChartsSource | `string?` | `` | URL of the ECharts UMD bundle to load. Falls back to the library-bundled version when not set. |
| Chart | AriaLabel | `string?` | `` | Accessible text alternative for the chart (WCAG 1.1.1). When set, the canvas host is exposed as role="img" with this aria-label so screen readers announce a summary instead of an empty graphic. Strongly recommended. |
| Chart | AccessibilityLayer | `bool` | `true` | When true (default, matching shadcn's accessibilityLayer), the chart renders a visually-hidden data table built from the series data and makes the canvas host keyboard-focusable with a summarising aria-label, so screen-reader and keyboard users can reach the otherwise opaque <canvas> data. When AriaLabel is not set, a generated summary of the data is used as the label. Set to false to opt out entirely. |
| Chart | MaxAccessibilityRows | `int` | `ChartAccessibility.DefaultMaxAccessibilityRows` | Upper bound on the number of data rows the hidden screen-reader table materialises (default DefaultMaxAccessibilityRows). A big series would otherwise emit one hidden <tr> per data point — thousands of rows of DOM bloat that no assistive tech can usefully navigate. Above the cap the table keeps the first N rows plus a single "… and N more data points" row; the host's summarising aria-label is built from the FULL data so its totals stay accurate. Set to 0 (or negative) to disable the cap and render every row. |
| Chart | Width | `string` | `"100%"` | CSS width of the chart container. Defaults to 100%. |
| Chart | Height | `string` | `"350px"` | CSS height of the chart container. Defaults to 350px. |
| Chart | OnClick | `EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>` | `` | Invoked with the ECharts click-event payload when the user clicks a chart element. |
| Chart | OnMouseOver | `EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>` | `` | Invoked with the ECharts mouseover-event payload when the pointer hovers a chart element. |
| Chart | ShowLoading | `bool` | `false` | When true, shows ECharts' built-in loading spinner overlay — distinct from the skeleton driven by IsLoading. |
| Chart | Group | `string?` | `` | Chart group name used to sync cursor/zoom interactions across multiple charts sharing the same group. |
| Chart | AspectRatio | `string?` | `` | CSS aspect-ratio applied to the container instead of a fixed Height (e.g. 16/9). |
| Chart | OnDoubleClick | `EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>` | `` | Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user double-clicks a chart element. |
| Chart | OnBrushSelected | `EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>` | `` | Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user completes a brush selection. |
| Chart | OnDataZoom | `EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>` | `` | Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user zooms or pans via data zoom. |
| Chart | OnInitError | `EventCallback<string>` | `` | Invoked with the error message when ECharts fails to initialize (e.g. an invalid EChartsSource). |
| Chart | Class | `string?` | `` | Additional CSS class(es) applied to the chart's root element. |
| Chart | AdditionalAttributes | `Dictionary<string, object>?` | `` | Additional HTML attributes captured and forwarded to the chart's root element. |
| Chart | IsLoading | `bool` | `false` | When true, forces the loading state on top of the chart (consumer is refetching data). Independent of the JS-driven ECharts loading indicator controlled by ShowLoading. |
| Chart | ShowLoadingSkeleton | `bool` | `true` | When true (default), renders the loading state while the chart has not yet rendered for the first time, and whenever IsLoading is true. Set to false to opt out entirely. |
| Chart | SkeletonKind | `ChartSkeletonKind` | `ChartSkeletonKind.Generic` | Shape family for the skeleton. Each chart wrapper presets a sensible default (bars for Bar, pie ring for Pie, etc.) — consumers only override when they want a different silhouette. |
| Chart | SkeletonStyle | `ChartSkeletonStyle` | `ChartSkeletonStyle.Phantom` | Phantom (default) renders the real chart with synthesized placeholder data in a muted palette; ECharts animates the option swap to real data. Silhouette falls back to the legacy SVG overlay skeleton. |
| Chart | PhantomCycleMs | `int` | `1400` | Phantom-mode refresh interval in milliseconds — each tick regenerates the placeholder dataset with a new random seed so bars/lines/slices visibly redistribute while loading. Defaults to 1400ms; clamped to ≥200ms. |
| Chart | PhantomShapeOverride | `PhantomShape?` | `` | Explicit override for the phantom layout (category count, series count, stacking, area fill). When null (default), the shape is inferred from the real Option so the phantom mirrors the actual chart structure. |
| Chart | ShowLoadingLabel | `bool` | `true` | When true (default), overlays a small top-right pill containing a spinner and LoadingText on top of the active skeleton — a clear, always-on visual cue that the chart is loading. The pill renders independently of SkeletonStyle so phantom and silhouette both get the same hint. Set to false for bare visuals where the skeleton speaks for itself. |
| Chart | LoadingText | `string?` | `` | Label rendered next to the spinner inside the loading pill. Set to null or empty to hide the text and show only the spinner. When not set, uses the active locale's "Chart.Loading" string (default: "Loading…"). |
| AreaChart | Categories | `List<string>` | `new()` | Category labels along the axis, one per data point; series values are matched to these by index. |
| AreaChart | Series | `List<ChartSeriesData>` | `new()` | The series to plot, each with a name and its list of values aligned to Categories by index. |
| AreaChart | Smooth | `bool` | `false` | When true, renders the line using a smoothed (curved) interpolation instead of straight segments between points. |
| AreaChart | ShowDots | `bool` | `true` | When true (default), draws a marker dot at each data point on the line. |
| AreaChart | Stacked | `bool` | `false` | When true, stacks each series on top of the previous one instead of overlaying them independently. |
| AreaChart | Gradient | `bool` | `true` | When true (default) the fill uses a lighter opacity than a solid area. This is the legacy opacity-only behaviour and is unaffected by GradientFill. |
| AreaChart | GradientFill | `bool` | `false` | Opt into a real vertical gradient fill: each series' area fades from its own (brand/theme) colour at the top to transparent at the bottom, the look used by modern dashboards. Off by default so existing charts are unchanged. Override the stops with GradientStops. |

Offsets run 0 (top) → 1 (bottom); colours accept theme tokens like var(--color-primary). When null and GradientFill is true, a per-series colour → transparent fade is used. |

## Slots
- `ChildContent` (Chart) — Declarative children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Other content renders nothing visible.
- `ChildContent` (AreaChart) — Declarative annotation children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Forwarded to the underlying Chart.
- `ChildContent` (BarChart) — Declarative annotation children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Forwarded to the underlying Chart.
- `ChildContent` (ChartTooltip) — The tooltip body. Receives the current ChartTooltipContext for the hovered point. The fragment is re-rendered into the chart's hidden tooltip portal on every hover; ECharts pulls the portal's innerHTML into the tooltip box.
- `ChildContent` (LineChart) — Declarative annotation children — ChartThreshold and ChartReferenceZone. Forwarded to the underlying Chart.

## Events
- `OnClick` (`EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>`) — Invoked with the ECharts click-event payload when the user clicks a chart element.
- `OnMouseOver` (`EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>`) — Invoked with the ECharts mouseover-event payload when the pointer hovers a chart element.
- `OnDoubleClick` (`EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>`) — Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user double-clicks a chart element.
- `OnBrushSelected` (`EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>`) — Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user completes a brush selection.
- `OnDataZoom` (`EventCallback<ChartEventArgs>`) — Invoked with the ECharts event payload when the user zooms or pans via data zoom.
- `OnInitError` (`EventCallback<string>`) — Invoked with the error message when ECharts fails to initialize (e.g. an invalid EChartsSource).
